sections.paragraph:
ἀντλέω (ἄντλος, in Hom. ‘hold of a ship’, then ‘bilgewater’) fut. ἀντλήσω LXX; 1 aor. ἤντλησα (s. ἄντλημα; Theognis, Hdt. et al.).

① to draw a liquid from a source, draw, water understood (so Hdt. et al.; POxy 985; PLond III, 1177, 66 p. 183; LXX; TestAbr A; ParJer 2:5; Jos., Bell. 4, 472) w. acc. (Biogr. p. 428; Gen 24:13; Is 12:3) J 2:9; 4:7. Abs. (Hdt.6, 119; Diog. L. 7, 168 and 169) 2:8; 4:15.

② fig. endure (Trag. and later; Lucian, De Merc. Cond. 17 δουλείαν) πολλοὺς κόπους ἀ. Hs 5, 6, 2 (cod. A, s. ἀναντλέω). The fig. develops out of the idea of constantly drawing from a source, in this case troubles.—M-M. Spicq.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

Theognis Theognis , VI b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

acc. acc. = accusative

Biogr. Biogr. = Βιογραφοι, Vitarum scriptores Gr. minores, various dates—List 5

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 91
